query: section 1988 attorney fees standard of review abuse of discretion ca2

results.case_name: Arbor Hill Concerned Citizens v. County of Albany
results.holding: Established the 'reasonable paying client' standard for lodestar calculation in § 1988 fee awards. Standard of review: abuse of discretion for the award overall, de novo for legal conclusions.
key_quotes: We review a district court's award of attorneys' fees for abuse of discretion.
We review de novo the legal standard the district court applied in determining the fee award.
A district court should calculate the reasonable hourly rate by considering what a reasonable, paying client would be willing to pay.

results.holding: Abuse of discretion standard for § 1988 fee awards; district court abused discretion by failing to calculate lodestar before calculating reduction.
key_quotes: We review an award of attorneys' fees for abuse of discretion.
A district court abuses its discretion when it fails to apply the correct legal standard, which in the context of attorneys' fees means failing to start with the lodestar.

results.holding: Affirmed lodestar methodology; abuse of discretion standard applied to fee award, de novo to embedded legal questions.
key_quotes: An attorneys' fee award is reviewed for abuse of discretion. Legal conclusions underlying the fee award are reviewed de novo.
